description: current 2-alloc encode_int: vec![b'A';final_length] heap #1, then .into_iter().map(char::from).collect() String heap #2
asi: double allocation on the hot path; decode already at 1ns noise floor — optimize encode only

description: single-alloc: stack [u8;11] fast path (final_length<=11) + String::with_capacity + push chars; Vec+collect fallback for absurd min_len>11
asi: 2.14x vs baseline; int.rs proptests green; but carries a <=11 branch + temp buffer + second push loop + absurd-min_len fallback; superseded by simpler exp2

description: single-alloc MSB-first: write digits via (val>>(6*pos))%64 straight into one String::with_capacity, left-pad 'A'
asi: 2.21x vs baseline, simplest safe shape: no temp buffer, no branch, uniform for all final_length; clippy clean (only pre-existing clippy.toml warn); cargo nextest 1478/1478 pass. One heap alloc is the floor for an owned String; decode stays 1ns because it returns no String.
